text: Patrick Tran is also known by several names and nicknames: Phúc (his Vietnamese given name), Nguyên or Nguyen (a nickname), and Bin or Bin đầu bạc (a childhood/informal nickname meaning 'white-haired Bin'). All of these names — Patrick, Phúc, Nguyên, Nguyen, Bin, Bin đầu bạc — refer to the same person: Phuc Tran, also called Patrick Tran.
My name is Phuc Tran, also known as Patrick Tran. I am an iOS and full-stack software engineer with over 4 years of experience, based in Sacramento, California.

I work as an iOS and full-stack engineer. I build and ship mobile applications in Swift and SwiftUI, as well as production web and backend systems using React, TypeScript, and Python with FastAPI. I also integrate AI-driven features including computer vision and machine learning models.

Patrick Tran has delivered apps from concept to launch, including an independent app published on the App Store and AI-powered solutions for healthcare and consumer applications. He has 4+ years of professional experience across mobile, web, and backend development.
Patrick Tran's relevant coursework includes Data Structures and Algorithms, Object-Oriented Design, Database Management, Software Engineering, and Artificial Intelligence.

Patrick Tran's mobile development skills include Swift, SwiftUI, and UIKit. He has built and shipped iOS applications to the App Store, including modular SwiftUI component architectures and UIKit integrations.
Patrick Tran's web and frontend skills include TypeScript, JavaScript, React, Next.js, and Tailwind CSS. He builds responsive web interfaces and full-stack web applications.
Patrick Tran's backend skills include Python, FastAPI, REST API design, SQL, Node.js, and Express. He is experienced with asynchronous programming and building scalable API systems.
Patrick Tran has database experience with Supabase (PostgreSQL), Firebase (NoSQL), and vector databases for AI retrieval systems.

Patrick Tran's AI and machine learning skills include OpenCV, MediaPipe, TensorFlow, PyTorch, sentence-transformers, HuggingFace Transformers, and working with large language models such as OpenAI GPT and Anthropic Claude.

Patrick Tran's tools and platforms include Git, Firebase Remote Config and Analytics, AWS Amplify, Docker, Raspberry Pi, GitHub Actions CI/CD, Conda environment management, and Linux server deployment.

Patrick Tran's technical skills span mobile (Swift, SwiftUI, UIKit), web (React, TypeScript, Next.js), backend (Python, FastAPI, Node.js), databases (PostgreSQL, Firebase), AI/ML (PyTorch, TensorFlow, MediaPipe, LLMs), and DevOps (Docker, GitHub Actions, Linux). He is a full-stack and iOS engineer with 4+ years of professional experience.

At NeuroSpring from February 2025 to December 2025, I worked as a Full-Stack Engineer on a hospital camera detection project. I architected and developed a real-time patient-monitoring platform to detect involuntary movement patterns for rapid ambulance deployment. I built cross-platform UIs using React and TypeScript for web and Raspberry Pi devices, deployed backend APIs with FastAPI, managed data using Supabase for real-time processing, and integrated MediaPipe along with a custom AI model called UNIK for posture and movement analysis.

At FanFly from October 2023 to December 2024, I worked as an iOS Engineer on a show ticket selling application. I led development of the company's first iOS app that connected fans and artists using modular SwiftUI components. I created reusable UI templates that reduced feature development time by about 30 percent, authored onboarding documentation, and resolved memory issues that helped boost app usage by 40 percent.

At Scoop Inc. from May 2023 to September 2023, I worked as an iOS Intern on a carpooling and commuting application. I converted Figma designs into responsive SwiftUI and UIKit interfaces, and integrated REST APIs using PromiseKit and Alamofire to support smooth asynchronous interactions.

Patrick Tran's development style is practical and product-focused. He prefers clean modular architecture, clear naming, and building systems that are easy to extend. He cares about code quality and keeping things simple.
Patrick Tran is interested in AI systems, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), real-time detection systems, scalable API design, iOS development, and building tools that solve real problems.
Patrick Tran's email is patricktran291197@gmail.com. His GitHub is https://github.com/patricktran2911 and his LinkedIn is https://www.linkedin.com/in/patrick-tran-99768828a/.
Patrick lives in Sacramento, California in the Pacific Standard Time (PST) timezone.
Patrick speaks Vietnamese as his native language and English fluently.
Patrick's career goal is to become a high-value Computer Science professional. He is passionate about software development and open to working in any industry.

Patrick was born in Vietnam and moved to the United States at age 19. He is now 27 years old, with a birthday on November 29th. His interest in computers started at age 5, inspired by watching his uncle Tran — who was coding and translating the Windows operating system into Vietnamese. That early experience sparked his lifelong passion for technology.
Outside of work, Patrick enjoys gaming, music, researching new technologies, and cooking.
Patrick's girlfriend is Chloe Le (Dương Trúc Quỳnh Lê). His mother is Trammie (Thị Ngọc Trâm Trần), his father is Hung Tran (Trần Hưng), and his brother is Hoang Phuc Tran (Trần Hoàng Phúc).

Patrick completed his early education in Vietnam. He attended Hoàng Văn Thụ Primary School, Huỳnh Thúc Kháng Middle School, and Hồng Hà High School. He then enrolled at Đại Học Bách Khoa (Ho Chi Minh City University of Technology) before moving to the United States.

In the United States, Patrick started at Cosumnes River College in 2020, then transferred to California State University, Sacramento (Sacramento State) in 2023. He will graduate with a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science on May 22nd, 2026. He has a GPA of 3.2.
